<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# List all nodes by color

for n in nodes:
    c = n['color']
    by_color.setdefault(c, []).append(n)

for c in sorted(by_color.keys()):
    print(f"  {c}: {len(by_color[c])} nodes")
    for n in by_color[c]:
        print(f"    id={n['id']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary'][:3]}...")
</parameter>
</function>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try clicking on the gray shapes (G) near the bars
# id=1 at (9,27) - near green bar
# id=15 at (27,9) - near yellow bar
# id=11 at (19,42) - near top containers
# id=21 at (41,22) - near bottom containers

# First, let me check the exact boundaries
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['id'] in (1, 11, 15, 21):
        print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Try clicking on the gray shapes near the bars
actions = [
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 9, 'col': 27},   # gray shape near green bar
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 27, 'col': 9},   # gray shape near yellow bar
]
result = action(actions)
print(f"\nResult: {result}")

# Check what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame
before_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in before.segmentation['nodes']}
after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in after.segmentation['nodes']}

print("\nChanges:")
for nid in sorted(set(list(before_nodes.keys()) + list(after_nodes.keys()))):
    bn = before_nodes.get(nid)
    an = after_nodes.get(nid)
    if bn is None:
        print(f"  NEW id={nid}, color={an['color']}, pixels={an['pixels']}")
    elif an is None:
        print(f"  REMOVED id={nid}, color={bn['color']}, pixels={bn['pixels']}")
    elif bn['pixels'] != an['pixels']:
        print(f"  CHANGED id={nid}, color={bn['color']}, pixels={bn['pixels']}->{an['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
